---
title: Building Rust based R Packages
---

Welcome back! This afternoon we put the morning's Rust to work by building a
*real* R package powered by Rust.

We'll create a package called `{rurl}`—R bindings to the
[`url`](https://docs.rs/url) crate—so we can parse, inspect, and modify URLs at
the speed of Rust while feeling completely at home in R.

::: aside

The morning workshop ended on a `Point` struct with a few methods. We pick up
right there: our first two warm-up exercises port that work across the FFI
boundary before we touch the `url` crate.

:::

## Timeline

This is the conservative timeline for the workshop. The first hour is the most
"lesson"-heavy. Once we start building `{rurls}` it's almost entirely hands-on.

|Section                              |Duration | End time |
|:------------------------------------|:--------|:---------|
|Foreign Function Interfaces (FFI)    |0:10     |1:40 PM   |
|What is extendr?                     |0:10     |1:50 PM   |
|Workshop Scope                       |0:05     |1:55 PM   |
|Hello, world!                        |0:10     |2:05 PM   |
|Package structure                    |0:10     |2:15 PM   |
|Developer Experience                 |0:10     |2:25 PM   |
|Break                                |0:05     |2:30 PM   |
|Adding the `url` crate               |0:05     |2:35 PM   |
|R's Type System                      |0:10     |2:45 PM   |
|New types                            |0:10     |2:55 PM   |
|External pointers                    |0:10     |3:05 PM   |
|Vectorization                        |0:10     |3:15 PM   |
|NA handling                          |0:10     |3:25 PM   |
|A print helper                       |0:05     |3:30 PM   |
|Break                                |0:05     |3:35 PM   |
|Integrating with {vctrs}             |0:10     |3:45 PM   |
|Accessing Struct Methods from R      |0:10     |3:55 PM   |
|Modifying an `Option<T>`             |0:05     |4:00 PM   |
|🐮 CoWs and Modifying in place        |0:10     |4:10 PM   |
|Combining iterators                  |0:10     |4:20 PM   |
|Recycling & errors                   |0:10     |4:30 PM   |
